body_txt: What's better than killing your most hated enemy? Sending them into the afterlife with your voice in their ears and the proof of your badassery in their head, that's what. Another common staple of Action Heroes , Anti Heroes , and Villains , this finishing quote need not be a One-Liner , but the longer your little speech goes , the more likely the victim will find some way to evade your intended killing blow or worse, make you Talk to the Fist . Consequently, Genre Savvy heroes are advised to just Kill Him Already! and save the pithy quotes for immediately afterwards . Of course, sometimes (as in the Heroes and Matrix examples) it seems that a Pre-Mortem One-Liner somehow paralyzes your victim while you deliver it, or maybe they're just courteous. Alternatively, the quote can be said in that short window of time after the killing blow is struck, but before your enemy's consciousness fades, as a metaphorical way of adding salt to the wound. Be warned, however, that if your enemy still has motor control, the quote might spur them to pull off a Taking You with Me . Give My Regards in the Next World and See You in Hell are sub-tropes. The monstrous equivalent is less articulate. Also related but different is a one-liner delivered when escaping from battle . Compare and contrast with its Sister Trope , Pre-Asskicking One-Liner , which is used at the beginning of a fight or to end it non-lethally ; the Pre-Mortem One Liner is used to end the fight lethally . Also compare Bond One-Liner (aka Post-Mortem One-Liner), which is said after the victim's consciousness has faded, and directed at the audience, to another character, or just to oneself. If the victim is the one speaking, it's Defiant to the End or "Facing the Bullets" One-Liner , though they can overlap if the character is Taking You with Me .
